description: A polished DHS promotional graphic designed to look like an airline ticket / boarding pass. The top portion is a white ticket stub bordered by red rules, with a vertical barcode down the left edge and a perforated dashed edge on the right; the official DHS seal sits centered at the very top. In huge navy condensed sans-serif caps the ticket reads 'FREE TICKET' on the first line and an even larger 'HOME' on the second. Below, on a dark navy starfield/textured background, a yellow headline announces '$1,000 to Self-Deport,' followed by white body text: 'Non-criminal illegal aliens can access travel assistance on their own terms to avoid arrest and earn a stipend for returning home.' A bold white line states 'There is no excuse to not self deport.' At the bottom is a red pill-shaped call-to-action button reading 'Visit DHS.gov/CBPHome.' The clean red-white-and-blue corporate design and travel-ticket metaphor reframe deportation as a free, voluntary, even rewarded trip home. The messaging technique combines an incentive ($1,000 stipend) with an implied threat ('avoid arrest'), targeting undocumented immigrants to pressure self-deportation via the CBP Home app while signaling enforcement resolve to the broader public.
notable_text: FREE TICKET HOME | $1,000 to Self-Deport | There is no excuse to not self deport. | Visit DHS.gov/CBPHome
